provisions.description: Continuous, iterative risk management across full lifecycle including risk identification, estimation, evaluation, elimination/reduction/mitigation, residual risk acceptance, testing, and post-market monitoring integration

sub_provisions.description: Establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system consisting of a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system
Identification and analysis of the known and the reasonably foreseeable risks that the high-risk AI system can pose to health, safety, or fundamental rights

evaluation.message: At least one risk_register record is required for Art. 9 compliance — risk identification and analysis must be documented
At least one risk_treatment record is required — risk elimination, reduction, or mitigation measures must be documented
At least one management review record is required — Art. 9 mandates management review with signed minutes documenting decisions and linked risk IDs
Risk management evidence older than 1 year may not satisfy the continuous iterative lifecycle requirement of Art. 9(1)
A post-market monitoring plan attachment should be present — Art. 9(4) requires integration of post-market monitoring data into risk management
Every risk_register record must be linked to a subject entity to establish which AI system the risk applies to

provisions.description: Training, validation, and testing data shall be subject to data governance and management practices covering design choices, data collection processes, data preparation, bias examination, and gap identification

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — Art. 10 mandates documentation of data source, acquisition method, and chain of custody
At least one dataset_card record is required — Art. 10 requires documentation of dataset characteristics, representativeness, and known limitations
Every dataset_card must be linked to a dataset entity for proper traceability of data governance measures
Every data_provenance record must specify the source_uri for traceability of training data origin

provisions.normative_text_ref: EU AI Act Art. 11(1)-(2), Annex IV
provisions.description: Technical documentation shall be drawn up before the high-risk AI system is placed on the market or put into service and shall be kept up-to-date, demonstrating compliance with the requirements set out in this Chapter

evaluation.message: At least one evaluation_report record is required — Art. 11 and Annex IV require documented system performance evaluation methods and results
Evaluation reports should document the methodology used, per Annex IV requirements for system performance evaluation methods

provisions.description: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system, ensuring a level of traceability appropriate to the intended purpose

evaluation.message: At least one event_log record is required — Art. 12 mandates automatic recording of events throughout the system lifecycle
Every event_log record must have an event_type field — Art. 12 requires categorized logging for traceability
Every event_log record must have a correlation_id — Art. 12 requires traceability across related events and to human oversight actions
A logging_spec variant event_log record is recommended to document what the system logs and why, enabling deployers to interpret logs per Art. 12(1)
Event log records should be linked to a subject entity for traceability to the specific AI system

provisions.provision_name: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
provisions.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use it appropriately

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_disclosure record is required — Art. 13 mandates instructions for use enabling deployer compliance, including capabilities, limitations, and risk warnings
Transparency disclosures should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI system's capabilities and limitations are documented

provisions.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are used

evaluation.message: At least one human_oversight_action record is required — Art. 14 mandates design for effective human oversight including intervention, override, and stop capabilities
Human oversight actions should be linked to an actor entity to establish who performed the oversight

provisions.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle

evaluation.message: At least one evaluation_report record is required — Art. 15 mandates documented accuracy levels, robustness testing, and cybersecurity measures
Evaluation reports should include quantified metrics for accuracy assessment per Art. 15(1)

provisions.description: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall put a quality management system in place that ensures compliance with this Regulation, covering policies, procedures, and instructions spanning the full compliance lifecycle

evaluation.message: At least one governance_policy record is required — Art. 17 mandates a written quality management system with policies, procedures, and instructions
Quality management system governance_policy records should include attached policy documents (PDF or similar)

provisions.description: Providers of AI systems that generate synthetic audio, image, video, or text content shall ensure that the outputs are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — Art. 50(2) mandates machine-readable marking of synthetic content
Every transparency_marking record must specify marking_scheme_id — the marking standard or scheme used must be identifiable for interoperability verification
Every transparency_marking record must specify the content modality (text, image, audio, video, multimodal) for which marking is applied
Transparency marking records should specify the metadata_container used (e.g., C2PA manifest, EXIF, ID3) per Art. 50(2) interoperability requirements

provisions.description: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall provide technical documentation, a copyright policy compliant with Directive (EU) 2019/790, and a sufficiently detailed training data summary

evaluation.message: GPAI models must document training data provenance — Art. 53(1)(d) requires a sufficiently detailed summary of training data
GPAI models must document copyright compliance — Art. 53(1)(c) requires a policy to comply with EU copyright law including TDM opt-out mechanisms

A gpai_annex_xi_model_doc variant evaluation_report is recommended — Annex XI requires structured technical documentation including model architecture, training data characteristics, and compute resources
Data provenance records must specify source_type (licensed, scraped, public_domain, synthetic, user_generated) per Art. 53 training data summary requirements
